message:
fix(lab): improve network harness timing fidelity and gate test-only transport APIs

Distributed harness (lab/network/harness):
- Replace coarse tick-aligned fault execution with sub-tick precision:
  faults now fire at their exact scheduled timestamp by splitting each
  tick into segments bounded by the next fault event, ensuring that a
  fault at t=900ms is not delayed until the next full tick boundary
- Sort fault script events before the simulation loop and use a cursor
  to avoid O(N*F) rescanning on every tick
- Derive tick from NetworkConfig::tick_resolution via normalized_tick()
  so the harness and the underlying SimulatedNetwork stay in sync
- Store message envelopes with an optional retain_until deadline
  (StoredEnvelope) to support future message-expiry semantics
- Add tests: sub-tick fault timing accuracy, same-tick fault ordering
  (crash before restart within a single tick)

Simulated network (lab/network/network):
- Extract magic number 1000 into MAX_DUPLICATE_PACKET_DELAY and
  EXTRA_PACKET_DELAY_WINDOW_MICROS constants for clarity
- On HostCrash: drain the packet queue of all in-flight packets destined
  for the crashed host and decrement per-link in-flight counters, so a
  crash+restart cycle does not deliver stale pre-crash packets and does
  not permanently consume bandwidth quota
- Add tests: in-flight packets dropped on crash even after restart,
  dropped packets do not consume future bandwidth

Benchmarks (benches/*):
- Gate all RaptorQ-dependent benchmarks and helpers behind
  `#[cfg(feature = "test-internals")]` with no-op fallbacks, matching
  the new transport mock visibility gate and allowing `cargo bench`
  to compile without the test-internals feature
- Gate SimNetwork integration tests in transport_frontier_benchmark_contract
  behind the same feature flag

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
